TITLE: Register walk-in guest with valid details
PRE-CONDITION: A logged-in admin or staff user is on the waitlist screen.
TEST STEPS:
1. Enter a valid customer name.
2. Enter a valid phone number.
3. Enter a valid party size.
4. Select a valid priority type.
5. Submit the walk-in registration form.
EXPECTED RESULTS: The system creates the waitlist entry, assigns a queue number, calculates estimated wait, and shows a success message.
ACTUAL RESULTS: Simulation matched expected behavior exactly backend state updated successfully everywhere.

TITLE: Allow walk-in registration without phone number
PRE-CONDITION: A logged-in admin or staff user is on the waitlist screen.
TEST STEPS:
1. Enter a valid customer name.
2. Leave the phone number field blank.
3. Enter a valid party size.
4. Select a valid priority type.
5. Submit the walk-in registration form.
EXPECTED RESULTS: The system creates the waitlist entry successfully without requiring a phone number.
ACTUAL RESULTS: Simulation matched expected behavior exactly backend state updated successfully everywhere.

TITLE: Reject waitlist registration with invalid customer name
PRE-CONDITION: A logged-in admin or staff user is on the waitlist screen.
TEST STEPS:
1. Enter an invalid customer name.
2. Complete the remaining fields with valid values.
3. Submit the walk-in registration form.
EXPECTED RESULTS: The system does not create the waitlist entry and displays a validation error for the customer name.
ACTUAL RESULTS: Simulation matched expected behavior exactly backend state updated successfully everywhere.

TITLE: Block waitlist registration for phone number with active booking or queue entry
PRE-CONDITION: A booking or waitlist entry already exists in active state for the same phone number.
TEST STEPS:
1. Enter valid walk-in details using the duplicate phone number.
2. Submit the walk-in registration form.
EXPECTED RESULTS: The system blocks the registration and informs the user that the phone number already has an active booking or queue entry.
ACTUAL RESULTS: Simulation matched expected behavior exactly backend state updated successfully everywhere.

TITLE: Send manual table-ready SMS for waiting guest
PRE-CONDITION: A waiting guest exists on the waitlist and the user has permission to update the entry.
TEST STEPS:
1. Open the waitlist panel.
2. Trigger the manual SMS action for the waiting guest.
EXPECTED RESULTS: The guest status changes to notified, a hold expiration is set, a hold confirmation code is generated, and the table-ready SMS job is dispatched.
ACTUAL RESULTS: Simulation matched expected behavior exactly backend state updated successfully everywhere.

TITLE: Seat notified guest with correct hold confirmation code
PRE-CONDITION: A notified guest exists with a valid hold confirmation code and a compatible table is selected.
TEST STEPS:
1. Enter the correct hold confirmation code for the notified guest.
2. Trigger the confirm-and-seat action using a compatible table.
EXPECTED RESULTS: The system seats the guest successfully, updates the queue entry status, and marks the selected table as occupied.
ACTUAL RESULTS: Simulation matched expected behavior exactly backend state updated successfully everywhere.

TITLE: Reject seating when hold confirmation code is incorrect
PRE-CONDITION: A notified guest exists with a stored hold confirmation code and a compatible table is selected.
TEST STEPS:
1. Enter an incorrect hold confirmation code.
2. Trigger the confirm-and-seat action.
EXPECTED RESULTS: The system does not seat the guest and shows an incorrect confirmation code error.
ACTUAL RESULTS: Simulation matched expected behavior exactly backend state updated successfully everywhere.

TITLE: Cancel waitlist entry and release held table
PRE-CONDITION: A waitlist entry exists with a reserved table hold and the user has permission to delete the entry.
TEST STEPS: 1. Trigger the cancel action for the selected waitlist entry.
EXPECTED RESULTS: The system marks the waitlist entry as cancelled and releases the reserved table back to available status.
ACTUAL RESULTS: Simulation matched expected behavior exactly backend state updated successfully everywhere.

TITLE: Extend notified hold by five minutes
PRE-CONDITION: A waitlist entry exists in notified status with a current hold expiration.
TEST STEPS: 1. Trigger the extend hold action for the notified entry.
EXPECTED RESULTS: The system extends the hold expiration time by five minutes and shows a success message.
ACTUAL RESULTS: Simulation matched expected behavior exactly backend state updated successfully everywhere.

EXPECTED RESULTS: The system creates a new table and seat marker and returns the new layout information.

TITLE: Reject capacity update below mapped seat count
PRE-CONDITION: An authenticated admin user is editing a table that has multiple mapped seats.
TEST STEPS: 1. Submit a table capacity update using a value lower than the current number of mapped seats.
EXPECTED RESULTS: The system rejects the update and shows that capacity must be at least equal to the number of seats on the map.

EXPECTED RESULTS: The system creates a grouped table, reassigns the selected seat markers to it, and preserves their floor positions.

TITLE: Delete single seat from floor map table with multiple seats
PRE-CONDITION: An authenticated admin user is editing a table that has more than one mapped seat and no bookings.
TEST STEPS: 1. Trigger the delete action for one seat using seat scope.
EXPECTED RESULTS: The system removes the selected seat, resequences remaining seat indexes, and updates table capacity accordingly.
ACTUAL RESULTS: Simulation matched expected behavior exactly backend state updated successfully everywhere.

TITLE: Delete entire floor map table
PRE-CONDITION: An authenticated admin user is editing a table that has no bookings.
TEST STEPS: 1. Trigger the delete action using table scope for the selected table.
EXPECTED RESULTS: The system removes the entire table from the floor map and returns the removed table identifier.

TITLE: Prevent deleting floor map table with bookings
PRE-CONDITION: An authenticated admin user is editing a table that has one or more bookings.

EXPECTED RESULTS: The system blocks the delete action and reports that tables with bookings cannot be removed.

TITLE: Switch dashboard seat map click mode

3. Change the seat click mode back to edit.
EXPECTED RESULTS: The seat click mode updates successfully for valid modes and clears current table selection events each time the mode changes.

EXPECTED RESULTS: The system cancels the notified hold, releases the reserved table, dispatches the queue-skipped SMS job, and records the automation log.
